summary: Backend testing of reporting consistency fixes for Mulya Salon POS. All 12 new tests + 7 regression tests pass (19/19, 100%). Verified Jakarta TZ-aware filtering, single/multi/month/custom/future date ranges, /api/reports/validate-totals consistency, and cross-checks between dashboard, transaction report, and commission report.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: _build_date_query and _aggregate_transaction_summary are correctly used as single source of truth for both dashboard and reports - good DRY.

date_utils.py: clean module, ZoneInfo Asia/Jakarta used consistently; day_range_utc_iso covers full inclusive day (00:00:00.000000 -> 23:59:59.999999 Jakarta -> UTC ISO). Correct fix for the previous lexicographic string-compare bug.

/api/reports/validate-totals: useful self-diagnostic endpoint cross-checking raw, aggregation, and stylist-grouped pipelines; correctly flags untracked_legacy when transactions exist without stylist_id (expected per problem statement).
Minor: in /api/reports/transactions, .to_list(10000) hard cap may silently truncate for large salons over time - consider pagination later.
Minor warning: backend_test.py::test_create_transaction_with_stylist_and_commission returns a value (legacy code); pytest warns. Non-blocking.
seed_data_creation: Test fixture `ensure_today_transaction` creates one today transaction via POS flow if none exists, so cross-checks can be performed. Cleanup not required (real production-shape data).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reporting consistency suite at /app/backend/tests/test_reports_consistency.py covers: validate-totals (no params/single-day/future), single-day not-zero regression (lexicographic bug), multi-day, month, custom range, summary total_commission field, dashboard<->report cross-checks (count, revenue, commission), and Jakarta TZ scope. All 19 backend tests pass. Frontend was not tested per request (backend-only).
rca of the issue: Previous bug: report endpoints did $gte/$lte against raw 'YYYY-MM-DD' strings while DB stored full ISO datetimes like '2026-06-02T11:02:04.544677+00:00'. Lexicographically, '2026-06-02' <= '2026-06-02T11:02:04...' is true for $gte but $lte '2026-06-02' excluded all same-day transactions. Fix in date_utils.parse_date_range_utc_iso converts a Jakarta calendar date to inclusive UTC ISO range covering 00:00:00 to 23:59:59.999999 local time, ensuring all same-day records are included. Validated by test_single_day_not_zero and test_single_day_includes_all_transactions.
